Title: Multiple GmWRI1s are redundantly involved in seed filling and nodulation by regulating plastidic glycolysis, lipid biosynthesis and hormone signalling in soybean (Glycine max)

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/EREBP family; WRI1/AP2 DNA-binding protein with AP2 domains
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates genes involved in plastidic glycolysis, fatty acid biosynthesis and TAG assembly; elevates early nodulation genes; modulates auxin-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PKP; PPC; PDHC; KAS; BCCP; ACP; ACBP; LPAAT; PDCT
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Carbon partitioning, plastidic glycolysis, fatty acid biosynthesis, TAG assembly, nodulation-associated lipid metabolism, hormone signaling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing seeds, roots and nodules; highest in cotyledons; peaks at seed filling stages 4-5; increases during nodule development
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S (constitutive) for overexpression in Arabidopsis and soybean hairy roots; native proGmWRI1a for GUS expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation via binding AW-box cis-elements in target promoters
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bradyrhizobium japonicum USDA110 inoculation; rhizobial infection
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Not established; discussion suggests possible dimerization/partner-dependent activity
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in developing seeds and nodules; relatively high in leaves at early stages
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmWRI1a is a nucleus-localized AP2 TF highly expressed in developing seeds and nodules. 2. Overexpression increases TAG accumulation and decreases soluble sugars/starch, indicating enhanced carbon flux to lipids. 3. It directly binds AW-box-containing promoters of plastid glycolysis and fatty-acid/TAG genes and strongly transactivates most of them. 4. Overexpression increases nodule number and up-regulates early nodulation genes, whereas knockdown of GmWRI1s reduces nodulation and lipid accumulation. 5. It also modulates auxin-related gene expression and hormone levels, linking metabolism to nodulation.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator coordinating conversion of carbohydrates into fatty acids and storage/membrane lipids during seed filling and nodulation
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Alters soluble sugar and starch pools; pleiotropic effects on hormone signaling may affect developmental programs
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes TAG and membrane lipid accumulation, stimulates starch breakdown/glycolysis, and increases nodule number
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq on transgenic soybean hairy roots; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage oil for seed filling; membrane and symbiosis-associated lipids supporting nodulation and rhizobial interaction
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in Arabidopsis and soybean hairy roots; rescue of Arabidopsis wri1 mutant; RNAi knockdown in soybean hairy roots; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase transactivation; metabolite profiling; GUS expression

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/EREBP family; WRI1/AP2 DNA-binding protein with AP2 domains
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates genes involved in plastidic glycolysis, fatty acid biosynthesis, TAG assembly, nodulation-related genes, and auxin-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Carbon partitioning, plastidic glycolysis, fatty acid biosynthesis, TAG assembly, nodulation-associated lipid metabolism, auxin and jasmonate signaling
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in developing seeds, pods, roots and especially nodules; increases during seed filling and peaks in mature nodules
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S (constitutive) for overexpression in Arabidopsis and soybean hairy roots; native proGmWRI1b for GUS expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PKP; PPC; PDHC; KAS; BCCP; ACP; ACBP; LPAAT; PDCT; auxin-related genes including GmGH3, GmPIN, GmARF, GmAUX1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct transcriptional activation via AW-box binding for metabolic genes; modulation of hormone-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Bradyrhizobium japonicum USDA110 inoculation; rhizobial infection
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Not established; discussion suggests possible requirement for legume-specific partners or dimerization
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: More abundant than GmWRI1a in most tissues, particularly nodules
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmWRI1b is a nucleus-localized AP2 TF and a major WRI1 homolog highly expressed in seeds and especially nodules. 2. Overexpression raises TAG, phospholipids and galactolipids, lowers sugars/starch, and increases nodule number. 3. It binds AW-box-containing promoters of glycolytic and lipid-biosynthetic genes and up-regulates these targets in hairy roots. 4. It affects expression of nodulation genes and auxin/jasmonate-related genes, with accompanying changes in IAA, IAA-Asp, JA and JA-Ile levels. 5. Together with GmWRI1a it acts redundantly but shows distinct regulatory properties.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive regulator linking carbon reallocation to lipid synthesis and nodulation outcome
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduces soluble sugars and starch; perturbs auxin and jasmonate balance with possible broader developmental effects
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes TAG/membrane lipid accumulation and nodulation; modulates hormone homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq on transgenic soybean hairy roots; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage and symbiosis-supporting lipids for seed filling, membrane biogenesis and nodule development
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in Arabidopsis and soybean hairy roots; rescue of Arabidopsis wri1 mutant; RNAi knockdown of GmWRI1s; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase transactivation; metabolite and hormone profiling; GUS expression

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Alternative-spliced AP2/EREBP WRI1 isoform lacking VYL motif
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds promoters of glycolysis and lipid-biosynthetic genes but shows weaker transactivation than full-length GmWRI1a/b
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Carbon partitioning, glycolysis and lipid biosynthesis with reduced activity relative to full-length isoforms
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected as alternative splicing product; detailed endogenous pattern NA
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PKP; PPC; PDHC; KAS; BCCP; ACP; ACBP; LPAAT; PDCT
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct DNA binding with attenuated transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Discussion proposes possible heterodimerization with GmWRI1a/b
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GmWRI1b' is an alternative-spliced isoform lacking the conserved VYL motif. 2. It localizes to the nucleus and can bind AW-box-containing promoters of metabolic target genes. 3. It promotes TAG accumulation in soybean hairy roots but is less effective than full-length GmWRI1a/b and does not increase TAG in Arabidopsis wild-type seeds. 4. It only partially complements the Arabidopsis wri1 mutant and has reduced transactivation activity. 5. The isoform likely represents an attenuated regulatory variant contributing to flexibility of WRI1 function.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Partial, attenuated positive regulator of lipid biosynthesis rather than the major determinant
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced activity may dampen pathway activation; possible dominant-negative/heterodimer effects proposed but unproven
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Weakly promotes TAG accumulation and partially restores wri1 mutant defects
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression in Arabidopsis and soybean hairy roots; mutant complementation; qRT-PCR; yeast one-hybrid; dual-luciferase transactivation; lipid profiling
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Alternative splicing deletes a 9-bp exon encoding VYL in the first AP2 domain; activity is compromised relative to full-length proteins
